reasoning: The U.S. legislative process for major technology regulation is notoriously slow, as demonstrated by the years-long stalled efforts to pass comprehensive data privacy or antitrust laws. Given the knowledge cutoff (late 2025), there are less than two months remaining for Congress to pass, and the President to sign, a substantive AI regulation bill. Even if there is a typo in the prompt's cutoff and there are 13 months remaining, 2025 will be the start of a new Congress (119th) whose calendar will be heavily dominated by time-critical fiscal issues (e.g., expiring tax cuts, debt ceiling) and executive branch appointments. While there are some targeted AI bills and state-level initiatives (which eventually could spur industry to lobby for federal preemption), drafting, negotiating, and passing a comprehensive AI framework with binding requirements and mandatory standards by January 1, 2026, is highly unlikely. Therefore, the probability is strongly weighted toward "No".
